lesson_meta.source: 原文｜《友谊前的恐怖事件》(改编) · Adapted
lesson_meta.quiz_version: v8 (干扰项翻新:字数拉平+偏误化+位置配平 2026-05)

story.title: 恐怖事件
paragraphs: "我最讨厌蒋妍了！"这句话憋在我心里很久了！
蒋妍是我的室友。虽然她学习很勤奋，但是她的一些"坏习惯"，让我们几个和她同住一个宿舍的人都不堪其扰。她每晚都苦读到十二点，刺眼的灯光让我们无法入睡。更让人讨厌的是，我们每天清晨都被她的闹钟吵醒！
我们都委婉地提醒她尊重彼此的作息，但她始终没有什么改变。其他两位室友不愿多说，我却觉得一股气憋在心里，无处发泄。有一天早晨，美梦再次被闹钟吵醒，我忍无可忍，朝她吼道："蒋妍，你真的很讨厌！这宿舍不是你一个人的！"
"我——"蒋妍看着我怒气冲冲的样子，一句话也不敢反驳。
这以后，原本没有什么交流的两个人彻底成了"冤家"。本来我们住在同一个宿舍，抬头不见低头见，现在却谁也不搭理谁，各走各的路。直到一个周六晚上……
那天，两个室友回家了，留下我们俩共处一室。我们各自做事，谁也不理谁。宿舍中的空气仿佛凝固起来，房间里安静得可以听到彼此心跳的声音。忽然，门外传来扭动门把手的轻响。我和蒋妍都吓了一跳，同时朝门的方向望去。门缓缓裂开一条缝，一个人影站在门外，看不清样子，像是一个长发披散的女生。
奇怪的是，那女生没有吭声，也没有继续推门，而是微微仰起脸扫了我们一眼。我看得很清楚，她表情古怪地冲我笑了一下，随即掩上门。
我和蒋妍飞快地对视了一眼，在彼此的眼睛里都看到了恐惧。
我鼓起勇气拉开门，小心地探出身望了望，走廊空荡荡的，什么也没有。我锁上门，冲蒋妍摇了摇头。过后，我们赶紧熄灯上床。
熄灯后大约半小时，黑暗中，蒋妍问了一句："你看见了吗？"
"我看见了。好可怕啊！"我的声音有些颤抖。
她说："我也看见了。你觉得那是什么？她怎么笑得那么诡异？"我隐约看见蒋妍蜷缩在被子里瑟瑟发抖。
蒋妍接着说："其实我挺喜欢看鬼故事的，可没想到真有恐怖事件发生时，居然这么可怕……"
"你也喜欢读鬼故事？"我打断她的话。
真没想到，那一夜，我竟然和蒋妍聊了个通宵。我们发现彼此的共同点还挺多的，蒋妍也为之前的事情跟我道了歉。第二天，我把两本恐怖小说借给蒋妍，心中那个"讨厌鬼"早就不见了。
几天后，我在宿舍楼下遇到那个"女鬼"。原来她是新来的同学，就住在楼上，那天走错了宿舍。多么惊悚而美丽的误会啊！
因为这起"恐怖事件"，我和蒋妍变成了好朋友。其实，人与人相处，总会出现这样那样的问题。很多时候，只要多点交流，就可以解决这些问题，人们之间的关系也会变得更和谐。

抒情句.en: Emotive sentence — author directly expresses feelings about people/events (often uses "!")

议论句.en: Commentary sentence — author shares opinion or general truth based on events

记叙文.en: Narrative essay — a story-based text type

修辞手法.en: Rhetorical device — special language technique (hyperbole, metaphor, etc.) for stronger writing

夸张.en: Hyperbole — deliberate exaggeration for emphasis or dramatic effect

比喻.en: Metaphor/simile — comparing one thing to another to make it vivid

拟人.en: Personification — giving human qualities to non-human things

反讽.en: Irony — saying the opposite of what you mean, or a situation that contradicts expectation

反差.en: Contrast — sharp difference used to highlight something

双关.en: Pun / double meaning — one word carrying two layers of meaning at once

言外之意.en: Implied meaning — what the writer really means "between the lines", beyond the literal words

主题.en: Theme — the central idea or main message of a text

中心思想.en: Central message — the main point the author wants to convey

铺垫.en: Foreshadowing / setup — earlier details that prepare for later events

神态描写.en: Expression/facial description — how a person looks (eyes, face)

动作描写.en: Action description — what a person does (verbs, movements)

语言描写.en: Dialogue description — what a person says or how they speak

心理描写.en: Psychological description — what a person thinks or feels inside

人物描写.en: Character description — techniques used to portray a character

矛盾修辞.en: Oxymoron — putting two opposite words together (e.g. "terrifying yet beautiful")

俗语.en: Idiom / common saying — a fixed phrase with a special meaning

感叹号.en: Exclamation mark (!) — often signals strong emotion

引号.en: Quotation marks 「」— often signal special meaning, irony, or emphasis

脉络.en: Storyline structure — how a text is organised from start to end

冤家.en: "Enemies" — but used playfully for people who bicker yet are actually close

不堪其扰.en: Cannot stand the disturbance any more

抬头不见低头见.en: "Always bumping into each other" — you see them constantly, can't avoid

冷战.en: Cold war — refusing to talk to each other

忍无可忍.en: Cannot endure any longer; reached the breaking point

phaseIntro.title: 案件简报：先看全局
phaseIntro.titleEn: BRIEFING — See the Whole Picture First
phaseIntro.desc: 在深入分析前，侦探总要先掌握全案的来龙去脉。请你先通读全文，然后回答 5 个全局问题——理清文章脉络，看清故事如何一步步走向<term>主题</term>。
phaseIntro.descEn: Before going deep, every detective grasps the whole case first. Read the full text, then answer 5 big-picture questions to see how the story unfolds toward its theme.

questions.prompt: 梳理一下文章脉络：请把每个板块和它对应的段落连起来。
questions.promptEn: Map the structure: match each paragraph range to what it does in the story.

options: 故事背景：介绍人物关系，矛盾出现
事情的起因：发生恐怖事件
事情的经过：两人开始交流
事情的结果：讨厌鬼不见了
作者的看法：文章中心思想
故事背景：介绍人物关系，矛盾出现
事情的起因：发生恐怖事件
事情的经过：两人开始交流
事情的结果：讨厌鬼不见了
作者的看法：文章中心思想
故事背景：介绍人物关系，矛盾出现
事情的起因：发生恐怖事件
事情的经过：两人开始交流
事情的结果：讨厌鬼不见了
作者的看法：文章中心思想
故事背景：介绍人物关系，矛盾出现
事情的起因：发生恐怖事件
事情的经过：两人开始交流
事情的结果：讨厌鬼不见了
作者的看法：文章中心思想
故事背景：介绍人物关系，矛盾出现
事情的起因：发生恐怖事件
事情的经过：两人开始交流
事情的结果：讨厌鬼不见了
作者的看法：文章中心思想

questions.explanation: 这就是这篇课文的"骨架"：背景→起因→经过→结果→作者的看法。记叙文一般都按这条线展开。掌握了脉络，等于拿到了破案的整张地图。
questions.explanationEn: This is the "skeleton" of the text: Background → Trigger → Development → Resolution → Author's view. Most narratives follow this pattern. Map it, and you have the whole case at a glance.

questions.prompt: 故事的逻辑线：从开头到结尾，发生了什么变化？请补上中间的关键步骤。
questions.promptEn: The story's logic line: from start to end, what changed? Fill in the key middle steps.

chain.text: 一开始：「我」讨厌蒋妍（关系不和谐）· In the beginning: "I" hate Jiangyan (bad relationship)

options: 因为蒋妍换了新室友
因为有人在宿舍偷东西
因为彼此缺乏交流

chain.text: 发生恐怖事件（女鬼站门口）· The horror incident (the "ghost" at the door)

options: 两个人因为害怕开始交流
两个人都跑去告诉其他人
两个人决定搬出宿舍
chain.hint: 恐怖事件后两人发生了什么？

options: 永远不再说话
更生气，吵了一架
发现彼此有共同点，互相道歉

chain.text: 关系变得和谐，成为好朋友

questions.explanation: 这条逻辑链就是全文的"心脏"——「没交流→有矛盾→开始交流→化解矛盾→关系和谐」。作者用这个故事证明：多点交流，就能解决问题。这条逻辑链直接通向文章中心。
questions.explanationEn: This logic chain is the HEART of the text: No communication → Conflict → Start talking → Resolve conflict → Harmony. The author uses this story to prove: more communication solves problems. This chain leads directly to the central message.

questions.prompt:
记叙文中的<term>抒情句</term>，是作者对故事中的人或事抒发感情的句子（常用感叹号）。
下面哪一句是这篇课文的抒情句？
questions.promptEn: An **emotive sentence** is where the author expresses feelings about people or events (often with "!"). Which is THE emotive sentence here?
sentences: "蒋妍，你真的很讨厌！这宿舍不是你一个人的！"
多么惊悚而美丽的误会啊！
我们发现彼此的共同点还挺多的。
sentencesEn: "Jiangyan, you're so annoying! The dorm isn't just for you!"
What a terrifying yet beautiful misunderstanding!
We found we actually had many things in common.
questions.explanation: A 句是"我"对蒋妍说的话（人物对话）；C 句是事实描述。只有 B 句是作者站出来对整件事抒发感慨——这才是真正的<term>抒情句</term>。注意"多么……啊"这个结构和感叹号，是抒情句的典型标志。
questions.explanationEn: A is "I" speaking to Jiangyan (dialogue); C is just stating a fact. Only B is the AUTHOR stepping out to express feelings — that's an emotive sentence. The pattern "多么……啊" (How... !) with "!" is the typical sign.

questions.prompt:
记叙文中的<term>议论句</term>，是作者针对故事中的人或事发表看法、讲道理的句子。
下面哪一句是这篇课文的议论句？
questions.promptEn: A **commentary sentence** is where the author shares opinion or general truth based on events. Which is it?
sentences: 很多时候，只要多点交流，就可以解决这些问题，人们之间的关系也会变得更和谐。
更让人讨厌的是，我们每天清晨都被她的闹钟吵醒。
蒋妍也为之前的事情跟我道了歉。
sentencesEn: Often, with more communication, these problems can be solved, and relationships become more harmonious.
Even more annoying — every morning we got woken up by her alarm clock.
Jiangyan also apologised for what happened before.
questions.explanation: B 和 C 句都在讲"故事内容"——前者写讨厌的原因，后者写蒋妍的动作。只有 A 句作者跳出故事，发表了自己的"看法"——这就是议论句。议论句往往直接说出文章的中心思想，是阅读理解的关键钥匙。
questions.explanationEn: B and C just narrate events. Only A is where the author STEPS OUT to share a general view — that's a commentary sentence. It often states the central message directly and is the key to reading comprehension.

questions.prompt: 综合前面找到的抒情句和议论句，再结合整个故事——这篇课文的中心思想是什么？
questions.promptEn: Combining the emotive and commentary sentences with the whole story — what is the central message?
options: 宿舍生活很危险，会遇到奇怪的事，要小心防范
人和人相处难免有矛盾，多点交流就能化解
同学之间要互相帮助，一起做功课才能进步
optionsEn: Dorm life is dangerous; strange things happen, so stay alert.
Friction between people is normal; more communication resolves it.
Classmates should help one another and study together to improve.
questions.explanation: 中心思想要从整个故事提炼，不能只抓某个细节。A 抓住了"恐怖"这个表面情节（以偏概全），但全文重点不是吓人，是关系的化解。C 听起来很正面，却偏离了课文——文中两人不是靠一起做功课变好的，是靠那晚的交流。只有 B 对应了结尾的议论句。
questions.explanationEn: The main idea must come from the WHOLE story. A grabs the surface 'horror' detail (over-generalising); C sounds positive but strays from the text. Only B matches the closing commentary sentence.

quiz.phaseName: 第二案·档案一：人物反应

phaseIntro.title: 档案一：人物为什么这么反应？
phaseIntro.titleEn: FILE 1 — Why do they react this way?
phaseIntro.desc: 读懂故事，先读懂人。课文里的"我"、蒋妍、其他室友，他们为什么会有那些反应？这关考你对人物心理的洞察力。
phaseIntro.descEn: To understand a story, first understand the people. Why does each character react the way they do? This case tests your insight into character psychology.

questions.prompt:
第 2 段："虽然她学习很勤奋，但是她的一些「坏习惯」……"

蒋妍学习勤奋是好事，为什么"我"却把她的习惯叫做<term>"坏习惯"</term>（注意有引号）？
questions.promptEn:
Para 2: "Although she studies hard, some of her 「bad habits」..."

Studying hard is good — so why does "I" call her habits "bad habits" (with quotation marks)?
options: 蒋妍是故意养成这些习惯，专门来气宿舍里的人
这些习惯对蒋妍未必算坏，只是影响了同住的人
这些习惯本来就很坏，迟早会拖累蒋妍的成绩
optionsEn: Jiangyan formed these habits on purpose, to annoy her roommates.
The habits aren't really bad for her — they just affect those living with her.
The habits are genuinely bad and will eventually hurt her grades.
questions.explanation: 关键在引号。"坏习惯"打了引号，说明这不是真正的坏——苦读、定闹钟对蒋妍自己其实是好事，只是影响到了别人。A 把"影响别人"误读成"故意害人"（张冠李戴动机）；C 只看"坏"字面、忽略引号（读不出反讽）。
questions.explanationEn: The quotation marks are the key. 'Bad habits' in quotes signals they aren't truly bad — they only affect others. A misreads the motive; C ignores the irony of the quotes.

questions.prompt:
第 3 段："其他两位室友不愿多说，我却觉得一股气憋在心里，无处发泄。"

为什么其他室友选择沉默，而"我"却忍不下这口气？
questions.promptEn:
Para 3: "The other two roommates didn't want to say more, but I felt anger trapped in my chest..."

Why do the others stay silent while "I" cannot hold it in?
options: 其他室友内向、怕冲突，而"我"性子直、憋不住
其他室友其实根本没有被蒋妍的种种坏习惯影响到
其他室友更喜欢蒋妍，所以才站出来保护她
optionsEn: The others are introverted and avoid conflict; 'I' am blunt and can't hold it in.
The other roommates simply weren't affected by her habits at all.
The other roommates like Jiangyan more, so they protect her.
questions.explanation: 课文说大家都"委婉提醒"过她，可见都被影响了，差别只在性格：别人忍着不爆发，"我"忍不住。B 与课文矛盾（无中生有——大家明明都提醒过）；C 凭空想象了一个"保护"动机（张冠李戴，文中没有任何依据）。
questions.explanationEn: The text says everyone hinted at her politely — so all were affected; the difference is personality. B contradicts the text; C invents a 'protection' motive with no basis.

questions.skillTagText: 能力点·人物反应（开放思考）
questions.prompt: 第 4 段："「我——」蒋妍看着我怒气冲冲的样子，一句话也不敢反驳。" 蒋妍被"我"突然这样大吼，她"一句话也不敢反驳"。她心里真的服气吗？请说说你的看法，写 2-3 句话。
questions.promptEn: Para 4: After being shouted at, "Jiangyan didn't dare to talk back." Did she TRULY agree? Share your view in 2-3 sentences.
questions.sampleAnswer: 蒋妍未必真的服气，但被"我"的凶气势吓到了，所以一句话不敢反驳。她心里可能又委屈又吃惊——没想到自己的习惯影响了别人这么深，所以一时不知道说什么。
questions.sampleAnswerEn: Jiangyan probably didn't fully agree but was scared by I's fierce attitude. She may have felt both wronged and shocked — she didn't realize her habits affected others so much, and didn't know what to say.
questions.hint: 想一想：蒋妍是真的认错了，还是被"我"的气势吓到了？她"不敢反驳"和"心里服气"是同一回事吗？可以从蒋妍的性格和当时的处境两个角度想。

questions.prompt:
第 17 段："真没想到，那一夜，我竟然和蒋妍聊了个通宵。"

之前两人是<term>冷战</term>的"<term>冤家</term>"，为什么会突然聊一通宵？
questions.promptEn:
Para 17: "I never expected — that night, Jiangyan and I actually chatted all night."

They were "enemies" yesterday — why suddenly chat all night?
options: 恐怖事件让两人都害怕，作伴时聊出了共同点
蒋妍先主动道了歉，"我"心一软就原谅了她
两人都不愿再吵下去，于是各自主动放下了成见
optionsEn: The horror scared both; needing company, they chatted and found common ground.
Jiangyan apologised first, and 'I' softened and forgave her.
Neither wanted to keep fighting, so each let go of the grudge.
questions.explanation: 要分清原因和结果的先后。那晚能聊起来，是因为共同的恐惧逼她们说话——这是原因。道歉、放下成见是聊开之后的结果。B、C 都把结果当成了原因（因果倒置）：不是先道歉才聊，是先因为害怕聊起来，才有了后面的道歉。
questions.explanationEn: Sort out cause and effect. They talked because shared fear forced them to — that's the cause. The apology and letting go are the result. B and C reverse cause and effect.

quiz.phaseName: 第二案·档案二：人物描写

phaseIntro.title: 档案二：作者怎么"画"人物？
phaseIntro.titleEn: FILE 2 — How does the author "paint" characters?
phaseIntro.desc: 好的作者从不直接说"这个人很害怕"，而是写她的眼神、动作、声音让你感受到害怕。这关学会看出"描写"背后的目的。
phaseIntro.descEn: Good writers never say "she's scared". They write her eyes, actions, voice so you FEEL the fear. This case teaches you to see the purpose behind descriptions.

questions.prompt: 课文里有许多描写"我"和蒋妍恐惧的细节。请把下面的句子和它属于哪种描写连起来。
questions.promptEn: Match each sentence to its TYPE of description.

rows.left: "在彼此的眼睛里都看到了恐惧"
options: 神态/眼神描写
动作描写
声音/语言描写
身体描写

rows.left: "我鼓起勇气拉开门，小心地探出身……锁上门……赶紧熄灯上床"
options: 神态/眼神描写
动作描写
声音/语言描写
身体描写
神态/眼神描写
动作描写
声音/语言描写
身体描写

rows.left: "蒋妍蜷缩在被子里瑟瑟发抖"
options: 神态/眼神描写
动作描写
声音/语言描写
身体描写

questions.explanation: 作者用了四种不同角度来写恐惧：眼神（看到恐惧）、动作（鼓起勇气、小心、赶紧）、声音（颤抖）、身体（蜷缩、发抖）。这就是好作者的功夫——不直接说"害怕"，而是从各个角度让你"看见"害怕。
questions.explanationEn: The author uses four different angles to show fear: eyes, action, voice, body. That's a good writer's craft — never say "scared" directly; show it from every angle.

questions.prompt: 为什么作者要费这么多笔墨写两人的恐惧（眼神、动作、声音、身体都写）？
questions.promptEn: Why does the author spend so much ink describing their fear?
options: 写足恐惧，后面两人因恐惧抱团交流才可信
作者主要是想吓唬读者，让读者读了也跟着害怕
写恐惧是为了证明那晚真的有女鬼来过宿舍
optionsEn: Building enough fear makes the later bonding-through-fear believable.
The author mainly wants to scare readers into feeling afraid too.
The fear proves a real ghost truly came to the dorm that night.
questions.explanation: 记叙文里每处描写都为情节服务。把恐惧写够，是为了让后面"两人因害怕而抱团、开始交流"显得真实——这是铺垫。B 只看到"吓人"这一层表面效果（抓细节当目的）；C 把"渲染气氛"误当成"证明灵异是真的"，可结尾揭示那只是误会（误读虚实）。
questions.explanationEn: In narrative, every description serves the plot. Writing enough fear makes the later bonding believable — it's foreshadowing. B sees only the surface scare; C mistakes atmosphere for proof of a real ghost.

questions.prompt:
第 4 段写蒋妍被吼时只说了"我——"两个字。作者为什么只让她说半个字，没让她说完整的话？

请说说这个<term>语言描写</term>的作用，写 2-3 句话。
questions.promptEn: In Para 4, Jiangyan only said "I——" after being shouted at. Why only half a word? What's the effect? Write 2-3 sentences.
questions.sampleAnswer: 只让蒋妍说"我——"两个字，写出了她被"我"突然爆发的气势吓到了，一时震惊得说不出话来。这种"欲言又止"比让她长篇大论解释，更能表现她当时的吃惊和委屈。
questions.sampleAnswerEn: Letting Jiangyan say only "I——" shows she was shocked by I's sudden burst of anger and was speechless. This "wanting to speak but unable to" expresses her shock and grievance better than a long explanation.
questions.hint: 想一想：如果蒋妍说了一整段话来解释自己，给人的感觉会一样吗？为什么作者要让她说不出话？这种"半句话"的描写在表达什么情绪？

quiz.phaseName: 第二案·档案三：修辞与句子

phaseIntro.title: 档案三：作者怎么写"出彩"？
phaseIntro.titleEn: FILE 3 — How does the author make writing "shine"?
phaseIntro.desc: 一句话可以平淡，也可以让人后背发凉——区别在于修辞手法和用词的精准。
phaseIntro.descEn: A sentence can be flat — or chill your spine. The difference is rhetoric and precise word choice.

questions.prompt:
课文写："宿舍中的空气仿佛凝固起来，房间里安静得可以听到彼此心跳的声音。"

作者这样写，最主要想让读者感受到什么？
questions.promptEn:
The text writes: "The air in the dorm seemed to freeze; it was so quiet you could hear each other's heartbeats."

What does the author most want the reader to FEEL here?
options: 房间静到了极点，那种安静本身就让人心里发毛
两人的心跳声真的特别大，大到能被对方清楚听见
宿舍隔音很差，平时连说话声都听得一清二楚
optionsEn: The room is utterly silent — and that silence itself feels eerie.
Their heartbeats are literally so loud each can really hear the other.
The dorm has poor soundproofing; even talking is always clearly heard.
questions.explanation: "安静得能听到心跳"是夸张——心跳当然不可能真被听见。作者用夸张，是要让你感到那种静到发毛的紧张。B 把夸张当成了事实，真以为心跳能听见（字面直解，这是读夸张句最常见的错）；C 脱离当晚情境，把"安静"误解成"隔音差"（乱推断）。
questions.explanationEn: 'So quiet you could hear heartbeats' is hyperbole — heartbeats can't truly be heard. It conveys an eerie, nerve-wracking silence. B takes the hyperbole literally; C ignores the context.

questions.prompt:
"宿舍中的空气仿佛凝固起来，房间里安静得可以听到彼此心跳的声音。"

请用 2-3 句话说说这两句<term>夸张</term>在文中起了什么作用。
questions.promptEn:
"The air seemed to congeal; you could hear each other's heartbeat."

In 2-3 sentences, explain what this hyperbole does in the text.
questions.sampleAnswer: 这两句夸张写出了宿舍气氛的凝重和安静，渲染了一种压抑的氛围。同时也突出了"我"和蒋妍冷战时互不搭理造成的尴尬，让读者感受到两人关系紧张到了顶点。
questions.sampleAnswerEn: The hyperbole conveys how heavy and silent the dorm feels, creating an oppressive atmosphere. It also highlights the awkwardness of the cold war — readers feel the relationship has reached peak tension.
questions.hint: 夸张写气氛——但这个气氛为什么要这么紧张？联系上文，是因为两人在冷战。所以这两句不只写气氛，还在突出两人的关系。

questions.prompt:
课文写："门缓缓<u>裂开</u>一条缝……"

如果把"裂开"改成"打开"，可以吗？
questions.promptEn:
The text says: "The door slowly cracked open a slit..."

Can we replace 「裂开」(crack open) with 「打开」(open)?
options: 可以，"裂开"和"打开"意思一样，都是门开了
不可以，"裂开一条缝"更形象，慢动作般添了紧张感
不可以，"打开"语气太平常，配不上这种恐怖的场面
optionsEn: Yes — 'crack open' and 'open' mean the same; the door opens either way.
No — 'crack open a slit' is more vivid, adding slow-motion tension.
No — 'open' sounds too ordinary to suit a scary scene.
questions.explanation: "打开"是中性动作，"裂开"让人联想到裂缝、被硬撑开，配上"缓缓""一条缝"，画面像慢动作恐怖镜头。A 只看词义相近，忽略了用词的表达效果（只比意思、不比效果）；C 方向对但理由空泛——不是"语气平常"的问题，而是"裂开"具体制造了什么画面，C 说不出来（知其然不知其所以然）。
questions.explanationEn: 'Open' is neutral; 'crack open' suggests a forced slit, and with 'slowly' becomes a slow-motion horror shot. A compares only meaning, not effect; C is vague about WHY.

questions.skillTagText: 能力点·句子在篇章中的作用
questions.prompt: 这些精彩的描写（夸张气氛、精准动词）在整篇课文中起什么作用？
questions.promptEn: What role do these vivid descriptions play in the WHOLE text?
options: 为"恐怖事件"铺垫，气氛够紧张事件才吓人
单纯为了把恐怖气氛写到位，让这一段更吓人
为了让读者以为真有女鬼，制造一个灵异的结局
optionsEn: They foreshadow the incident — only with peak tension does it feel scary.
Purely to render the horror well, making this passage scarier.
To make readers believe in a real ghost and set up a supernatural ending.
questions.explanation: 要看描写在整篇里的作用。这些渲染是铺垫：气氛紧张到极点 → 恐怖事件 → 两人因恐惧抱团交流 → 化解矛盾。B 只看到"让这段更吓人"的局部效果，没看到它为后文情节服务（抓细节当中心）；C 误判了文章走向，以为是灵异结局，其实是"美丽的误会"（误读情节）。
questions.explanationEn: Judge the description's role in the WHOLE text — it's foreshadowing leading to the turning point. B sees only the local scare; C misjudges the plot direction.

quiz.phaseName: 第二案·档案四：言外之意

phaseIntro.title: 档案四：词语的"言外之意"
phaseIntro.desc: 有些词，表面意思是 A，但作者真正想说的是 B。这关训练你的"听话听音"能力。
phaseIntro.descEn: Some words say A on the surface but really mean B. This case trains your "reading between the lines".

questions.skillTagText: 能力点·引号的言外之意
questions.prompt:
第 5 段：「原本没有什么交流的两个人彻底成了「<u>冤家</u>」。」

"冤家"加了引号，是什么<term>言外之意</term>？
questions.promptEn:
Para 5: "The two who barely talked became total 「enemies」."

With quotation marks, what does 「冤家」(enemies) really mean?
options: 就是字面意思的仇人，两人都恨不得对方立刻消失
应该是"朋友"写错了，作者用错了引号
字面是仇人，但引号说明不是真仇，带点亲近调侃
optionsEn: Literally enemies — each wishes the other would vanish at once.
It's a typo for 'friend'; the author misused the quotation marks.
Literally 'enemy', but the quotes show it's not real — a teasing closeness.
questions.explanation: "冤家"加引号，是要你别照字面理解。中文里"冤家"本就常用来说"表面闹别扭、其实关系特殊"的人。A 照字面把它当真仇人（读不出引号的言外之意）；B 把引号当成写错字（误判标点功能——引号不是用来标错字的）。
questions.explanationEn: 'Enemy' in quotes signals don't read it literally; in Chinese it often means people who bicker but are actually close. A takes it literally; B mistakes the quotes for marking a typo.

questions.prompt:
第 17 段：「心中那个「<u>讨厌鬼</u>」早就不见了。」

这里的"讨厌鬼"指的是？
questions.promptEn:
Para 17: "The 「annoying ghost」 in my heart was long gone."

What does "annoying ghost" refer to here?
options: 就指那个当晚真的看到的"女鬼"，如今已经消失不见了
指走错宿舍的新同学，误会解开后她就搬走了
一语双关——既指"女鬼"，也指心里对蒋妍的坏印象
optionsEn: Just the 'ghost' they really saw, which has now disappeared.
The new student who entered the wrong dorm; she moved away after.
A pun — both the 'ghost' and the bad impression of Jiangyan in my heart.
questions.explanation: "讨厌鬼"是双关：表面呼应那晚的"女鬼"，深层指"我"心里给蒋妍贴的负面标签。两个"鬼"都没了——误会解开，心结也解开。A 只抓住表层的"女鬼"（只读一层、漏掉深意）；B 把"讨厌鬼"误当成具体某个人（张冠李戴），其实它指的是"我"的心理。
questions.explanationEn: 'Pesky ghost' is a pun: the literal 'ghost' and the negative label on Jiangyan in my heart. Both 'ghosts' vanish. A catches only the surface; B mistakes it for a specific person.

questions.prompt:
第 18 段：「多么<u>惊悚而美丽</u>的误会啊！」

"惊悚"是吓人的，"美丽"是美好的——这两个意思相反的词放在一起，作者想表达什么？

请用 2-3 句话回答。
questions.promptEn: Para 18: "What a 「terrifying yet beautiful」 misunderstanding!" Why put two opposite words together? Answer in 2-3 sentences.
questions.sampleAnswer: "惊悚"指的是事情发生时两人都被吓得不轻；"美丽"指的是这件事的结果——让"我"和蒋妍化解矛盾，成为好朋友。作者把两个矛盾的词放一起，是想说：这个误会虽然过程吓人，但结果却是美好的。
questions.sampleAnswerEn: "Terrifying" refers to how scared they were during the event; "beautiful" refers to the result — it resolved conflict and made them friends. The author puts these opposite words together to say: although the process was scary, the outcome was beautiful.
questions.hint: 想一想：什么地方"惊悚"？什么地方"美丽"？这两件事分别指什么？再问问自己：作者为什么不直接说"美丽的误会"或"惊悚的误会"，偏要把两个词放一起？

questions.skillTagText: 能力点·俗语的言外之意
questions.prompt:
第 5 段：「本来我们住在同一个宿舍，<u>抬头不见低头见</u>。」

<term>抬头不见低头见</term>是什么意思？为什么作者要在这里用这个俗语？
questions.promptEn: Para 5: "We lived in the same dorm — 「抬头不见低头见」". What does this idiom mean? Why use it here?
options: 指两人不愿打招呼，总是故意躲着对方走开
指两人不愿打招呼，总是故意躲着对方走
形容两人天天碰面，作者借此突出冷战时的尴尬
optionsEn: It means they refuse to greet and always deliberately avoid each other.
It means they differ greatly in height, so their viewing angles differ.
It means they meet daily; the author uses it to stress the cold-war awkwardness.
questions.explanation: "抬头不见低头见"是俗语，意思是经常见面。作者用它，是为了反衬冷战的尴尬：明明天天碰面，却谁也不理谁。B 把意思读反了——这俗语说的是"常见面"，不是"躲着"（俗语理解颠倒）；C 望文生义，从"抬头""低头"猜成身高（字面拆解、不懂整体含义）。
questions.explanationEn: The idiom means they meet often. The author uses it to contrast with the cold-war awkwardness. B reverses the meaning; C reads it literally as a height difference.

quiz.phaseName: 第二案·档案五：作者观点

phaseIntro.title: 档案五：作者真正想说什么？
phaseIntro.titleEn: FILE 5 — What does the author truly mean?
phaseIntro.desc: 现在你已经找到了抒情句和议论句，也明白了文章主题。这关再深一步——看看作者为什么这么想，他的观点站得住脚吗？
phaseIntro.descEn: You've found the emotive and commentary sentences, and grasped the theme. Now go deeper — WHY does the author think this way?

questions.prompt: 这篇记叙文中，"我"和作者是什么关系？
questions.promptEn: What's the relationship between "I" and the author?
options: "我"是作者讨厌的某个真实人物，借故事来讽刺他
"我"很可能代表作者本人或作者想表达的视角
"我"是完全虚构的人物，和作者本人毫无关系
optionsEn: 'I' is a real person the author dislikes, satirised through the story.
'I' likely represents the author, or the viewpoint the author wants to express.
'I' is a wholly fictional character with no link to the author.
questions.explanation: 记叙文里第一人称"我"常常代表作者本人或作者的视角——证据是最后一段作者直接站出来发议论。A 凭空想象了一个"讽刺真实人物"的动机（无中生有）；C 走到另一个极端，说"完全无关"，但结尾的议论分明就是作者的心声（忽略关键证据）。
questions.explanationEn: First-person 'I' in narrative often represents the author or their viewpoint — the closing commentary is the evidence. A invents a satire motive; C ignores that closing voice.

questions.prompt: 作者的观点是"多点交流就能解决问题"。这个观点在课文里有什么依据支撑？
questions.promptEn: The author claims "more communication solves problems". What EVIDENCE in the text supports this?
options: 没什么真凭据，作者只是事后随便发了点感慨
依据是作者读过很多心理学方面的书，所以才这样认为
整个故事就是依据——亲身经历证明了交流能化解矛盾
optionsEn: No real evidence; the author just tosses out a casual remark afterward.
The evidence is that the author read many psychology books.
The whole story is the evidence — lived experience proves communication resolves conflict.
questions.explanation: 作者观点的依据，就藏在整个故事里："我"和蒋妍从冷战到和好，靠的正是那晚的交流——这是亲身经历的证明。A 否认有依据，可故事本身就是依据（看不出叙事在为观点服务）；B 凭空加了"心理学书"，课文里根本没提（无中生有）。
questions.explanationEn: The evidence is the whole story: the shift from cold war to friendship via that night's talk. A denies evidence though the story IS the evidence; B invents 'psychology books' never mentioned.

questions.prompt:
作者说：「只要多点交流，就可以解决这些问题。」

你赞同这个观点吗？为什么？（无论赞同或不赞同，请说出理由。）

请用 3-5 句话回答。
questions.promptEn: The author says: "With more communication, these problems can be solved." Do you agree? Why? Answer in 3-5 sentences.
questions.sampleAnswer: 我大致赞同。因为很多矛盾都是因为不了解对方的真实想法，一交流就发现是误会，就像课文里的"我"和蒋妍。但我觉得也不是所有问题都能靠交流解决——有些矛盾涉及根本利益冲突，光靠聊天可能不够，还需要双方愿意妥协。
questions.sampleAnswerEn: I mostly agree. Many conflicts come from not understanding each other — once you talk, you see it's a misunderstanding, like "I" and Jiangyan. But not ALL problems can be solved by talking — some involve real interest clashes; both sides must be willing to compromise.
questions.hint: 可以从两个角度想：1）支持的话，举一个生活中的例子；2）不完全支持的话，想想什么情况下"光交流"还不够？好的回答常常是"有条件的赞同"——既承认作者有道理，也提出自己的补充。

questions.prompt: 终极任务：联系自己的生活——你和同学（或朋友、家人）相处时，有没有遇到过类似的问题？你是怎么处理的？后来关系有变化吗？ 请讲一个具体的小故事，60-150 字。
questions.promptEn: Final mission: Connect to your life — have you faced a similar problem with classmates/friends/family? How did you handle it? Did the relationship change? Tell a specific short story, 60-150 chars.
questions.sampleAnswer: 我和同桌曾经因为我没借她笔而闹得很僵，整整一个星期不说话。后来一次小组作业，我们被分到一组，不得不交流。聊了几句才发现，她当时不是真的需要笔，只是想找个理由跟我说话。原来我们都是因为面子才冷战。现在我们成了好朋友——这跟课文里的"我"和蒋妍真的很像！
questions.sampleAnswerEn: My deskmate and I had a cold war for a week after I refused to lend her a pen. Later, in a group project we were forced to talk. After a few words, I realised she didn't really need the pen — she just wanted an excuse to talk. We were both being too proud. Now we're close friends — just like "I" and Jiangyan!
questions.hint: 想一个具体的场景——什么时候、和谁、为什么有矛盾、后来怎么解决的、现在关系怎么样？越具体越好，不要写"我和很多同学都吵过架"这种空话。可以参考课文里"我"和蒋妍的故事结构。
